diagrams.title: OSI vs TCP/IP Model Comparison
diagrams.description: A side-by-side comparison of the 7-layer OSI model and the 4-layer TCP/IP model, showing how the layers map to each other. The OSI model shows (from top to bottom): Application, Presentation, Session, Transport, Network, Data Link, Physical. The TCP/IP model shows: Application, Transport, Internet, Network Access. Arrows indicate which OSI layers correspond to each TCP/IP layer.
elements: 7 OSI layer boxes stacked vertically on the left
4 TCP/IP layer boxes stacked vertically on the right
Layer numbers and names clearly labeled
Color coding to distinguish different layers
diagrams.purpose: Helps students understand the relationship between the theoretical OSI model and the practical TCP/IP protocol suite, clarifying how network communication functions are organized across layers.

diagrams.title: Network Topologies
diagrams.description: Five common network topologies displayed as simple network diagrams: (1) Bus - single cable with nodes connected along it, (2) Star - central hub/switch with all nodes connected to center, (3) Ring - nodes connected in a circular path, (4) Mesh - nodes interconnected with multiple paths, (5) Tree - hierarchical structure with root and branches. Each topology shown as a small labeled diagram with nodes (circles) and connection lines.
elements: Bus topology with linear backbone cable
diagrams.purpose: Visualizes the different ways devices can be physically or logically arranged in a network, helping students understand the advantages and disadvantages of each topology configuration.

diagrams.title: Network Types by Geographic Area
diagrams.description: A hierarchical diagram showing network types organized by coverage area from smallest to largest. Each network type represented by a concentric zone or circular area: PAN (Personal Area Network) - within reach of one person, LAN (Local Area Network) - single building/campus, MAN (Metropolitan Area Network) - city/region, WAN (Wide Area Network) - worldwide. Includes example devices and approximate ranges for each type.
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the scale differences between network types, helping students understand when and why different network categories are used based on coverage area and purpose.
